task_completion.description: Login to Floreant POS using the provided PIN.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Floreant POS login screen with '1111' entered, or the main dashboard visible after successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the printer configuration section in the Back Office.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Back Office interface showing the 'Printers' or 'Printer Configuration' window.
A text field for the printer name containing exactly 'Kitchen-Expo-1'.

task_completion.description: Configure the printer as a Virtual Printer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown, radio button, or selection for printer type showing 'Virtual Printer' selected.

task_completion.description: Designate the printer for kitchen output.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox, dropdown, or list selection indicating the printer is assigned to 'Kitchen' or a similar kitchen routing option.
The 'Kitchen-Expo-1' printer appearing in the saved list of printers, or a confirmation dialog indicating the printer was successfully saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the Floreant POS GUI to configure the printer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Floreant POS application windows, not terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not manipulate the Apache Derby database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQL INSERT commands or database client interfaces (like ij or DBeaver) modifying the VIRTUAL_PRINTER table.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Floreant POS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window has the standard Floreant POS UI elements and branding, rather than a mock interface or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected printer name is exactly 'Kitchen-Expo-1'. The printer type must be set to 'Virtual Printer' in the Floreant POS GUI. The agent must use the Floreant POS GUI to complete the task, not direct database manipulation.
